overview.summary: Arrays store same-type values in contiguous memory, making index-based access extremely fast.
learningObjectives: Explain why random access is O(1).
Recognize why inserts and deletes in the middle cost more.
overview.interviewRelevance: Arrays are the default storage model for most interview problems.

paragraphs: Every pixel in an image, every row in a spreadsheet, and every daily temperature reading can be modeled as an array.
Arrays are the simplest mental model for “a list of values with positions.”
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: An array is powerful because the computer can jump straight to any position by arithmetic.

definition.technicalDefinition: An array is a fixed-size contiguous block of memory that stores values of the same type and supports direct index-based access.

vocabulary.definition: The zero-based position of a value in the array.
Stored in one continuous span of memory.
Directly jumping to an index without walking through earlier values.

keyProperties: Values sit next to each other in memory.
Access by index is constant time.
Middle insert/delete requires shifting later values.

metaphor: Picture a row of labeled lockers. Each locker has a number, and the label lets you jump to it instantly.
mentalModel.clickSentence: The value is easy to access because its address is base + offset.

memoryDescription: Arrays reserve one continuous memory region large enough for every element.
Because every slot has the same size, the address of index i can be calculated directly.

memoryLayout.label: base address
memoryLayout.value: where the array starts in memory

memoryLayout.label: element size
memoryLayout.value: how many bytes each value occupies

steps.explanation: Scan index 0. The value 4 is not the target yet.
Scan index 1. The value 8 is not the target yet.
Scan index 2. The value 15 is not the target yet.
Scan index 3. The value 16 is not the target yet.
We reached index 4 and found the target value 23.
Scan index 5. The value 42 is not the target yet.

operations.name: Access
operations.description: Compute the offset and jump directly to the requested index.
operations.visualNote: Only one cell is highlighted because direct access does not scan earlier values.

operations.name: Insert in Middle
operations.description: To insert in the middle, every later value shifts right by one slot.
operations.visualNote: Cells after the insertion point visibly shift right.

patternTemplate.pseudocode:
address = base + index * element_size
return memory[address]
patternTemplate.whenToUse: Use arrays when position matters and fast index lookup is more important than cheap middle insertions.
patternTemplate.timeComplexity: Access O(1), search O(n), insert/delete in middle O(n).

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: You need the fastest possible lookup by numeric position in a dense sequence.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: Arrays are the best fit when direct indexing is the core requirement.

examples.title: Linear Search in an Array
examples.problemStatement: Scan the array from left to right until you find the target.
steps: Compare each value to the target.
Stop when the target is found.
examples.rationale: In the worst case every element is compared.

examples.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
examples.problemStatement: Rewrite the array in-place so each distinct value appears once.

conceptQuiz.question: Why is array access O(1)?
conceptQuiz.explanation: The address calculation is arithmetic, not traversal.

conceptQuiz.question: Why is inserting in the middle expensive?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Contiguous layout means later elements must move to preserve order.

conceptQuiz.question: What is the biggest strength of arrays?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Arrays dominate when direct positional access matters most.

conceptQuiz.question: What is stored at an array index?
options: A value of the element type
conceptQuiz.explanation: Each slot contains one value of the array element type.

practice.title: Search Insert Position
practice.keyInsight: Indexed linear or binary reasoning depending on sortedness.

practice.title: Remove Element
practice.keyInsight: Rewrite in-place while preserving a valid prefix.

practice.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
practice.keyInsight: Track the best prior value while scanning forward.

practice.title: Move Zeroes
practice.keyInsight: Keep a compacted prefix of non-zero values.

practice.title: Merge Sorted Array
practice.keyInsight: Fill from the back to avoid overwriting values.

practice.title: Container With Most Water
practice.keyInsight: Array positions define the geometry of the answer.
